2026-04-20T22:39:20.2344227Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T22:39:20.2344658Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T22:39:20.2345017Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T22:39:20.2345314Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

2026-04-20T22:42:01.2181844Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T22:40:55.50161726Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"58408","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-20T22:42:01.2194753Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T22:40:55.661007746Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58408,\"title\":\"Remove duplicate CredentialSource required from Azure.Core credential schema\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nWhen a consumer references a client library that ships a `ConfigurationSchema.json` segment (via the per-package `buildTransitive` `.targets`), the appsettings schema combiner produces a combined `obj/{config}/{tfm}/AppSettingsSchema.json`. In that combined file, the `required` array on the merged `definitions/credential` ends up containing `CredentialSource` twice:\\n\\n```json\\n\\u0026#34;required\\u0026#34;: [\\n  \\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;,\\n  \\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;\\n]\\n```\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\n1. `dotnet pack` `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets` locally.\\n2. Reference the resulting nupkg from a fresh `net10.0` console project.\\n3. `dotnet build` the consumer.\\n4. Inspect `obj\\\\Debug\\\\net10.0\\\\AppSettingsSchema.json` — exactly one `required` array contains `CredentialSource` twice; 22 others contain it once.\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nBoth segments declare `required: [\\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;]` at the top of `definitions/credential`:\\n\\n- `sdk/core/System.ClientModel/schema/ConfigurationSchema.json` (line 13)\\n- `sdk/core/Azure.Core/schema/ConfigurationSchema.json` (formerly lines 124-126)\\n\\nThe combiner merges segments by definition name and concatenates their `required` arrays without dedup, producing the duplicate.\\n\\n## Fix\\n\\nRemove the top-level `required: [\\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;]` from `definitions/credential` in Azure.Core\\u0026#39;s schema. System.ClientModel still asserts it, so the combined schema\\u0026#39;s behavior is unchanged. All `if`-clause `required` arrays and the `chainableCredential` definition (which has no scm counterpart) are preserved.\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\nAfter repacking Azure.Core with this change and rebuilding the same consumer:\\n\\n- 23 `required` arrays with `CredentialSource` (unchanged total)\\n- **0 duplicates** (was 1)\\n\\n## Follow-up Suggestion\\n\\nThe schema combiner should dedup `required` arrays when merging same-named definitions across segments, since multiple segments can legitimately declare the same required key. This PR fixes the symptom on the segment side; a combiner-side fix would prevent recurrences for any segment pair.\",\"state\":\"closed\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/58408\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-nash\",\"id\":64171366,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/m-nash\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64171366?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Azure.Core\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-20T22:07:17Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-20T22:37:34Z\",\"closed_at\":\"2026-04-20T22:37:32Z\",\"closed_by\":\"m-nash\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-20T22:42:01.2217952Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T22:40:55.834370059Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58408,\"title\":\"Remove duplicate CredentialSource required from Azure.Core credential schema\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nWhen a consumer references a client library that ships a `ConfigurationSchema.json` segment (via the per-package `buildTransitive` `.targets`), the appsettings schema combiner produces a combined `obj/{config}/{tfm}/AppSettingsSchema.json`. In that combined file, the `required` array on the merged `definitions/credential` ends up containing `CredentialSource` twice:\\n\\n```json\\n\\u0026#34;required\\u0026#34;: [\\n  \\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;,\\n  \\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;\\n]\\n```\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\n1. `dotnet pack` `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets` locally.\\n2. Reference the resulting nupkg from a fresh `net10.0` console project.\\n3. `dotnet build` the consumer.\\n4. Inspect `obj\\\\Debug\\\\net10.0\\\\AppSettingsSchema.json` — exactly one `required` array contains `CredentialSource` twice; 22 others contain it once.\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nBoth segments declare `required: [\\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;]` at the top of `definitions/credential`:\\n\\n- `sdk/core/System.ClientModel/schema/ConfigurationSchema.json` (line 13)\\n- `sdk/core/Azure.Core/schema/ConfigurationSchema.json` (formerly lines 124-126)\\n\\nThe combiner merges segments by definition name and concatenates their `required` arrays without dedup, producing the duplicate.\\n\\n## Fix\\n\\nRemove the top-level `required: [\\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;]` from `definitions/credential` in Azure.Core\\u0026#39;s schema. System.ClientModel still asserts it, so the combined schema\\u0026#39;s behavior is unchanged. All `if`-clause `required` arrays and the `chainableCredential` definition (which has no scm counterpart) are preserved.\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\nAfter repacking Azure.Core with this change and rebuilding the same consumer:\\n\\n- 23 `required` arrays with `CredentialSource` (unchanged total)\\n- **0 duplicates** (was 1)\\n\\n## Follow-up Suggestion\\n\\nThe schema combiner should dedup `required` arrays when merging same-named definitions across segments, since multiple segments can legitimately declare the same required key. This PR fixes the symptom on the segment side; a combiner-side fix would prevent recurrences for any segment pair.\",\"state\":\"closed\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/58408\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-nash\",\"id\":64171366,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/m-nash\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64171366?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Azure.Core\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-20T22:07:17Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-20T22:37:34Z\",\"closed_at\":\"2026-04-20T22:37:32Z\",\"closed_by\":\"m-nash\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-20T22:42:01.2245489Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T22:41:47.643508643Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Core`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\n**Triggered by:** PR #58408 by @m-nash\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #58408 fixed a bug in `sdk/core/Azure.Core/schema/ConfigurationSchema.json` where the top-level `definitions/credential` declared `required: [\"CredentialSource\"]` — the same property already required by `System.ClientModel`'s schema segment. When the appsettings schema combiner merges segments by definition name, it concatenates `required` arrays without deduplication, producing `[\"CredentialSource\", \"CredentialSource\"]` in the consumer's combined `AppSettingsSchema.json`. The fix removes the duplicate declaration from Azure.Core's schema segment.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- The `### Bugs Fixed` section under `## 1.54.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` in `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md` is empty — there is no entry documenting this fix.\n- The `README.md` does not describe the AppSettings / `appsettings.json` IntelliSense schema feature at all (it was introduced in 1.52.0), so users encountering the duplicate-`required` issue have no documentation to reference.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**File:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md`\n**Action:** Edit — add a `Bugs Fixed` entry under `## 1.54.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)`.\n\nReplace the empty `### Bugs Fixed` section:\n\n```markdown\n### Bugs Fixed\n```\n\nwith:\n\n```markdown\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n- Fixed `ConfigurationSchema.json` credential definition to remove a duplicate top-level `required: [\"CredentialSource\"]` entry. When multiple schema segments are merged into `AppSettingsSchema.json`, the combiner concatenates `required` arrays without deduplication, producing `[\"CredentialSource\", \"CredentialSource\"]` in the combined file. Because `System.ClientModel` already asserts this requirement, removing it from Azure.Core's segment eliminates the duplicate with no change in validation behavior. ([#58408](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/58408))\n```\n\n**File:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md`\n**Action:** Edit — add a section under `## Key concepts` (after the existing credential bullet) explaining the AppSettings JSON schema feature. Insert the following after the line about `DefaultAzureCredential`/`ManagedIdentityCredential` and before the `### Thread safety` heading:\n\n```markdown\n### Configuring clients via `appsettings.json` (IntelliSense schema)\n\nStarting with Azure.Core 1.52.0, the NuGet package ships a `ConfigurationSchema.json` segment. When a consumer references Azure.Core (directly or transitively), the build tooling merges these segments into `obj/{config}/{tfm}/AppSettingsSchema.json`. This enables:\n\n- **IntelliSense / autocompletion** for `AzureClients` configuration blocks in `appsettings.json` inside Visual Studio and VS Code.\n- **Validation** of `Retry`, `Diagnostics`, and `CredentialSource` options at edit time.\n\nNo additional setup is required — the schema is automatically merged during `dotnet build`.\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nNo new executable snippets are required for this change. The CHANGELOG entry and README prose update above are sufficient.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` currently contains all required sections. No structural changes are needed beyond the new prose paragraph added in Step 1.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/core/Azure.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 core` — verify no unintended README changes.\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 core` — no public API changes expected; verify the output is unchanged.\n5. `dotnet format sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","title":"[Core] Docs: Azure.Core 1.54.0-beta.1 CHANGELOG missing AppSettings schema bug fix entry"},"name":"create_issue"}}}
